title: Treating Depression with Transcutaneous Auricular Vagus Nerve Stimulation: State of the Art and Future Perspectives
keyFinding: Transcutaneous auricular VNS shows promise as a non-invasive treatment for depression, with evidence of antidepressant effects through modulation of brain networks including the default mode network.

title: Vagus nerve stimulation therapy for partial-onset seizures: a randomized active-control trial
keyFinding: High-stimulation VNS produced a significantly greater reduction in seizure frequency (28%) compared to low-stimulation controls (15%) in drug-resistant epilepsy.

title: Effect of transcutaneous auricular vagus nerve stimulation on major depressive disorder: a nonrandomized controlled pilot study
keyFinding: Four weeks of taVNS significantly reduced Hamilton Depression Rating Scale scores compared to sham, with a response rate of 37.5% versus 12.5% in controls.

title: Noninvasive vagus nerve stimulation as acute therapy for migraine: the randomized PRESTO study
keyFinding: Non-invasive cervical VNS was safe and well-tolerated for acute migraine treatment, though the primary endpoint of pain-free status at 2 hours did not reach significance versus sham.

title: Vagus nerve stimulation inhibits cytokine production and attenuates disease severity in rheumatoid arthritis
journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences
keyFinding: Implanted VNS significantly inhibited TNF production and improved clinical signs in rheumatoid arthritis patients, providing direct evidence of the inflammatory reflex in humans.

title: Safety of transcutaneous auricular vagus nerve stimulation (taVNS): a systematic review and meta-analysis
keyFinding: Across 177 studies and 6,322 subjects, taVNS was generally well-tolerated with mild and transient side effects. Serious adverse events were rare and not clearly attributable to stimulation.

title: Effect of Transcutaneous Vagus Nerve Stimulation at Auricular Concha for Insomnia: A Randomized Clinical Trial
keyFinding: Four weeks of taVNS significantly improved Pittsburgh Sleep Quality Index scores and sleep efficiency in patients with chronic insomnia compared to sham stimulation.

title: Vagus nerve stimulation for drug-resistant epilepsy: a European long-term study up to 24 months in 347 children
keyFinding: Long-term VNS in paediatric epilepsy showed progressive improvement over 24 months, with 32% of patients achieving at least 50% seizure reduction.

title: Transcutaneous Vagus Nerve Stimulation Modulates Default Mode Network in Major Depressive Disorder
keyFinding: taVNS modulated functional connectivity in the default mode network and showed antidepressant effects, suggesting a central mechanism for its therapeutic action in depression.

title: Vagus nerve stimulation paired with rehabilitation for upper limb motor function after ischaemic stroke (VNS-REHAB): a randomised, blinded, pivotal, device trial
keyFinding: VNS paired with rehabilitation significantly improved upper limb motor function compared to rehabilitation with sham stimulation in chronic stroke patients at 90 days.

title: Non-invasive Vagal Nerve Stimulation Effects on Hyperarousal and Autonomic State in Patients with Posttraumatic Stress Disorder and History of Mild Traumatic Brain Injury: Preliminary Evidence
keyFinding: Non-invasive vagal nerve stimulation reduced PTSD symptom severity and showed promising effects on autonomic regulation in veterans with combat-related PTSD.

title: Chronic vagus nerve stimulation in Crohn's disease: a 6-month follow-up pilot study
keyFinding: Chronic VNS improved clinical and biological markers of Crohn's disease in 5 of 7 patients over 6 months, supporting the role of the cholinergic anti-inflammatory pathway.

title: Transcutaneous vagus nerve stimulation reduces spontaneous but not induced negative thought intrusions in high worriers
keyFinding: taVNS reduced spontaneous negative thought intrusions in high worriers compared to sham stimulation, suggesting vagal afferent pathways may modulate anxiety-related rumination.

title: Autonomic regulation therapy via left or right cervical vagus nerve stimulation in patients with chronic heart failure: results of the ANTHEM-HF trial
keyFinding: Chronic VNS was safe and improved left ventricular ejection fraction, heart rate variability, and six-minute walk distance in patients with symptomatic heart failure.

title: Direct and Transcutaneous Vagus Nerve Stimulation for Treatment of Tinnitus: A Scoping Review
keyFinding: Both direct and transcutaneous VNS show potential for tinnitus treatment, with evidence suggesting that VNS combined with sound therapy may reduce tinnitus severity.

title: Vagus nerve stimulation for epilepsy: a meta-analysis of efficacy and predictors of response
keyFinding: VNS reduced seizure frequency by at least 50% in approximately half of patients with drug-resistant epilepsy, with greater efficacy observed in generalised versus partial seizures.

title: The Impact of Auricular Vagus Nerve Stimulation on Pain and Life Quality in Patients with Fibromyalgia Syndrome
keyFinding: Auricular VNS was evaluated alongside exercise therapy in fibromyalgia patients, with improvements observed in select quality-of-life subparameters over 4 weeks of treatment.

title: Transcutaneous Cervical Vagal Nerve Stimulation in Patients with Posttraumatic Stress Disorder (PTSD): A Pilot Study of Effects on PTSD Symptoms and Interleukin-6 Response to Stress
keyFinding: Transcutaneous cervical VNS reduced PTSD symptom severity by 17% and attenuated interleukin-6 inflammatory responses to stress in patients with PTSD.

title: Non-invasive vagus nerve stimulation in healthy humans reduces sympathetic nerve activity
keyFinding: Transcutaneous VNS reduced muscle sympathetic nerve activity and shifted autonomic balance toward parasympathetic dominance in healthy volunteers, demonstrating a direct physiological mechanism for anxiolytic effects.

title: Effect of transcutaneous vagus nerve stimulation on muscle activity in the gastrointestinal tract (transVaGa)
keyFinding: Transcutaneous auricular VNS modulated gastrointestinal muscle activity as measured by electromyography, supporting its therapeutic potential for gastrointestinal motility disorders.

title: A 5-year observational study of patients with treatment-resistant depression treated with vagus nerve stimulation or treatment as usual: comparison of response, remission, and suicidality
keyFinding: Over 5 years, VNS plus treatment as usual produced significantly higher cumulative response rates (67.6% vs 40.9%) and remission rates (43.3% vs 25.7%) compared to treatment as usual alone in treatment-resistant depression.

title: Vagus-nerve stimulation for the treatment of epilepsy
keyFinding: Comprehensive review establishing VNS as an accepted treatment for refractory epilepsy, with efficacy that improves over 18-24 months and evidence of broad-spectrum antiseizure effects.

title: Effect of vagus nerve stimulation on serotonergic and noradrenergic transmission
journal: Journal of Pharmacology and Experimental Therapeutics
keyFinding: VNS significantly increased the basal firing rates of both serotonergic neurons in the dorsal raphe nucleus and noradrenergic neurons in the locus coeruleus, suggesting a novel antidepressant mechanism of action.

title: Vagus nerve stimulation increases norepinephrine concentration and the gene expression of BDNF and bFGF in the rat brain
keyFinding: Acute VNS increased the expression of BDNF and fibroblast growth factor in the hippocampus and cortex, and increased norepinephrine concentration in the prefrontal cortex, suggesting neuroplastic mechanisms.

title: Non-invasive access to the vagus nerve central projections via electrical stimulation of the external ear: fMRI evidence in humans
keyFinding: Electrical stimulation of the cymba conchae activated classical central vagal projections including the NTS, locus coeruleus, and dorsal raphe, providing fMRI evidence that vagal afferents can be accessed non-invasively via the external ear.

title: Auricular transcutaneous electrical nerve stimulation in depressed patients: a randomized controlled pilot study
keyFinding: Active auricular transcutaneous electrical nerve stimulation produced significantly greater improvement in Beck Depression Inventory scores compared to sham stimulation over two weeks in depressed patients.

title: The efficacy and safety of transcutaneous auricular vagus nerve stimulation in the treatment of depressive disorder: a systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials
keyFinding: Pooled analysis of RCTs found that taVNS significantly reduced depression scores compared to sham stimulation, with moderate effect sizes and fewer side effects than antidepressants.

title: Transcutaneous auricular vagus nerve stimulation for post-stroke depression: a double-blind, randomized, placebo-controlled trial
keyFinding: taVNS combined with conventional treatment significantly improved depression scores in post-stroke depression patients compared to sham, expanding potential applications beyond primary MDD.

title: Enhancement of the function of rat serotonin and norepinephrine neurons by sustained vagus nerve stimulation
keyFinding: Sustained VNS initially increases noradrenergic neuron firing (after 1 day) and subsequently serotonergic neuron firing (after 14 days), representing a unique cascade antidepressant mechanism.

title: Vagus nerve stimulation for treatment-resistant depression: a randomized, controlled acute phase trial
keyFinding: In the pivotal 10-week acute phase trial, active VNS produced a 15.2% response rate versus 10.0% for sham in treatment-resistant depression; the primary endpoint did not reach statistical significance.

title: The inflammatory reflex
keyFinding: Landmark review defining the 'inflammatory reflex' — a neural circuit by which the vagus nerve modulates the immune response, with cholinergic neurons inhibiting acute inflammation in real time.

title: Transcutaneous auricular vagus nerve stimulation modulating the brain topological architecture of functional network in major depressive disorder: an fMRI study
keyFinding: Four weeks of taVNS increased global efficiency and decreased characteristic path length in functional brain networks of MDD patients, with changes in network topology correlating with depression score improvements.

title: Optimization of transcutaneous vagus nerve stimulation using functional MRI
keyFinding: fMRI comparison of four ear stimulation locations found that the cymba conchae activated the vagal pathway most effectively, providing evidence for optimal taVNS electrode placement.

title: Effects of transcutaneous vagus nerve stimulation in individuals aged 55 years or above: potential benefits of daily stimulation
keyFinding: Two weeks of daily taVNS improved autonomic balance, baroreflex sensitivity, and some aspects of quality of life, mood, and sleep in adults aged 55 and over.

title: Transcutaneous auricular vagus nerve stimulation in insomnia: a systematic review and meta-analysis
keyFinding: Pooling data from six studies, taVNS significantly improved PSQI scores (MD = -3.60) and Insomnia Severity Index scores (MD = -5.24), with improvements across multiple sleep dimensions and minimal adverse effects.

title: Transcutaneous vagus nerve stimulation could improve the effective rate on the quality of sleep in the treatment of primary insomnia: a randomized control trial
keyFinding: One month of taVNS improved the effective rate of treatment for primary insomnia compared to sham, adding to evidence that taVNS produces clinically meaningful improvements in sleep quality.

title: Effects of transcutaneous auricular vagus nerve stimulation on brain functional connectivity of medial prefrontal cortex in patients with primary insomnia
keyFinding: taVNS altered functional connectivity of the medial prefrontal cortex in patients with primary insomnia, providing a neuroimaging correlate for the sleep-improving effects of vagal stimulation.

title: Transcutaneous auricular vagus nerve stimulation for chronic insomnia disorder: a randomized clinical trial
keyFinding: Active taVNS achieved a mean PSQI reduction of 8.2 points versus 3.9 for sham (Cohen's d = 1.2) in chronic insomnia, with benefits sustained over 20 weeks and no serious adverse effects.

title: Bilateral transcutaneous auricular vagus nerve stimulation for the treatment of insomnia in breast cancer
keyFinding: Nightly bilateral taVNS over two weeks significantly reduced insomnia severity, improved sleep quality, decreased cancer-related fatigue and depression, and increased HRV in breast cancer patients.

title: Vagus nerve stimulation attenuates the systemic inflammatory response to endotoxin
keyFinding: Landmark discovery that electrical stimulation of the vagus nerve dramatically reduced TNF production and attenuated systemic inflammation in an endotoxemia model, establishing the cholinergic anti-inflammatory pathway.

title: Splenectomy inactivates the cholinergic antiinflammatory pathway during lethal endotoxemia and polymicrobial sepsis
keyFinding: Splenectomy abolished the anti-inflammatory effect of cervical VNS, confirming that the spleen is the principal site of immune modulation in the cholinergic anti-inflammatory pathway.

title: Manipulation of the inflammatory reflex as a therapeutic strategy
keyFinding: Comprehensive review of how the inflammatory reflex operates through vagal and splenic nerve signalling to modulate macrophage cytokine production via alpha7 nicotinic receptors.

title: The cholinergic anti-inflammatory pathway
keyFinding: Established that the efferent vagus nerve inhibits pro-inflammatory cytokine release through acetylcholine signalling at alpha7 nicotinic receptors, defining the cholinergic anti-inflammatory pathway.

title: No consistent evidence for the anti-inflammatory effect of vagus nerve stimulation in humans: a systematic review and meta-analysis
keyFinding: Meta-analysis of 26 studies found no consistent evidence for anti-inflammatory effects of VNS in humans, highlighting the gap between compelling preclinical data and more equivocal clinical results.

title: Vagus nerve stimulation in autoimmune conditions: a systematic review
keyFinding: Across 12 clinical trials of VNS in autoimmune conditions, more than half found reductions in pro-inflammatory cytokines, with IL-6 most consistently reduced (6 of 7 studies).

title: Transcutaneous vagus nerve stimulation has no anti-inflammatory effect in diabetes
keyFinding: Transcutaneous cervical VNS did not reduce plasma concentrations of inflammatory cytokines (IL-6, IL-8, IL-10, TNF-alpha, IFN-gamma) in patients with diabetes over 56 days of treatment.

title: Nicotinic acetylcholine receptor alpha7 subunit is an essential regulator of inflammation
keyFinding: Established that the nicotinic acetylcholine receptor alpha7 subunit is essential for inhibiting cytokine synthesis by the cholinergic anti-inflammatory pathway, identifying the critical receptor on macrophages.

title: Functional and chemical anatomy of the afferent vagal system
keyFinding: Comprehensive anatomical review establishing that approximately 80% of vagal fibres are afferent, carrying sensory information from visceral organs to the brain, with the vagus innervating nearly the entire gastrointestinal tract.

title: The vagus nerve at the interface of the microbiota-gut-brain axis
keyFinding: Review establishing the vagus nerve as a key communication pathway between gut microbes and the brain, with vagal afferents sensing microbial metabolites and the cholinergic anti-inflammatory pathway modulating intestinal inflammation.

title: Stimulation of the vagus nerve attenuates macrophage activation by activating the Jak2-STAT3 signaling pathway
keyFinding: VNS ameliorated surgery-induced inflammation and post-operative ileus by activating STAT3 in intestinal macrophages through alpha7 nicotinic receptor-mediated Jak2-STAT3 signalling.

title: Relationship between vagal tone, cortisol, TNF-alpha, epinephrine and negative affects in Crohn's disease and irritable bowel syndrome
keyFinding: Vagal tone was inversely correlated with TNF-alpha in Crohn's disease and with epinephrine in IBS, supporting the relevance of vagus nerve reinforcement interventions in inflammatory bowel conditions.

title: Vagus nerve stimulation for treatment of partial seizures: 1. A controlled study of effect on seizures
keyFinding: In the first major controlled trial of VNS for epilepsy (E03), high-stimulation VNS achieved a 24.5% median reduction in seizure frequency versus 6.1% in the low-stimulation group in drug-resistant partial seizures.

title: Vagus nerve stimulation in 436 consecutive patients with treatment-resistant epilepsy: long-term outcomes and predictors of response
keyFinding: At mean 5-year follow-up, 63.8% of patients achieved at least 50% seizure reduction, demonstrating that VNS efficacy progressively improves over years of treatment in drug-resistant epilepsy.

title: Transcutaneous auricular vagus nerve stimulation (ta-VNS) for treatment of drug-resistant epilepsy: a randomized, double-blind clinical trial
keyFinding: At 20 weeks, the responder rate and relative seizure frequency reduction were significantly higher in the active taVNS group compared to controls, supporting non-invasive VNS for drug-resistant epilepsy.

title: Peripheral control of hypersynchronous discharge in epilepsy
keyFinding: Seminal preclinical demonstration that vagus nerve stimulation could suppress experimentally induced seizure activity, providing the scientific foundation for the development of clinical VNS therapy.

title: Transcutaneous cervical vagal nerve stimulation reduces sympathetic responses to stress in posttraumatic stress disorder: A double-blind, randomized, sham controlled trial
keyFinding: tcVNS attenuated sympathetic arousal during traumatic and mental stress in PTSD patients, decreasing heart rate and increasing peripheral vasodilation compared to sham over a three-day protocol.

title: Noninvasive Cervical Vagal Nerve Stimulation Alters Brain Activity During Traumatic Stress in Individuals With Posttraumatic Stress Disorder
keyFinding: tcVNS increased anterior cingulate and hippocampus activation during trauma scripts, potentially indicating a reversal of neurobiological changes associated with PTSD.

title: Effect of transcutaneous cervical vagus nerve stimulation on declarative and working memory in patients with Posttraumatic Stress Disorder (PTSD): A pilot study
keyFinding: Active tcVNS produced a 91% increase in paragraph recall performance by two months, suggesting VNS may improve declarative memory deficits associated with PTSD.

title: Preliminary evidence of transcutaneous vagus nerve stimulation effects on sleep in veterans with post-traumatic stress disorder
keyFinding: One hour of active taVNS at lights-out produced a moderate reduction in cyclic alternating pattern rate and increased parasympathetic activity during non-REM sleep in veterans with PTSD.

title: Transcutaneous vagal nerve stimulation blocks stress-induced activation of Interleukin-6 and interferon-gamma in posttraumatic stress disorder: A double-blind, randomized, sham-controlled trial
keyFinding: tcVNS blocked stress-induced increases in IL-6 and IFN-gamma in PTSD patients, demonstrating anti-inflammatory effects of vagal stimulation during traumatic stress exposure.

title: Non-Invasive Vagus Nerve Stimulation for the ACute Treatment of Cluster Headache: Findings From the Randomized, Double-Blind, Sham-Controlled ACT1 Study
keyFinding: nVNS response rates were significantly higher than sham for episodic cluster headache (34.2% vs 10.6%; P=0.008), though not for chronic cluster headache.

title: Non-invasive vagus nerve stimulation for the acute treatment of episodic and chronic cluster headache: A randomized, double-blind, sham-controlled ACT2 study
keyFinding: nVNS achieved pain-free status in 48% of episodic cluster headache attacks versus 6% with sham, confirming efficacy of gammaCore for episodic cluster headache.

title: Chronic migraine headache prevention with noninvasive vagus nerve stimulation: The EVENT study
keyFinding: First sham-controlled pilot study of nVNS for chronic migraine prevention; open-label completers showed approximately 7.9 fewer headache days after 8 months of sustained therapy.

title: Non-invasive vagus nerve stimulation (nVNS) for the preventive treatment of episodic migraine: The multicentre, double-blind, randomised, sham-controlled PREMIUM trial
keyFinding: Though the primary endpoint was not met, post hoc analysis of adherent patients showed significant benefit of nVNS for migraine prevention, with sham device inadvertently found to have parasympathetic activity.

title: Non-invasive vagus nerve stimulation for prevention of migraine: The multicenter, randomized, double-blind, sham-controlled PREMIUM II trial
keyFinding: Active nVNS achieved a 44.87% responder rate versus 26.81% for sham (P=0.048), with migraine with aura patients showing a therapeutic gain exceeding 100% over sham.

title: Vagus Nerve Stimulation Paired With Upper Limb Rehabilitation After Chronic Stroke: A Blinded Randomized Pilot Study
keyFinding: Active VNS group showed mean improvement of 9.5 FMA-UE points versus 3.8 in controls at day 90, with a clinically meaningful response rate of 88% versus 33% with control.

title: Vagus Nerve Stimulation Paired With Upper-Limb Rehabilitation After Stroke: One-Year Follow-up
keyFinding: At one-year follow-up, FMA-UE scores had increased by 9.2 points from baseline in the active VNS group, demonstrating durable long-term improvements with good treatment adherence.

title: Vagus Nerve Stimulation Paired With Rehabilitation for Upper Limb Motor Impairment After Chronic Ischemic Stroke: Subgroup Analysis of the VNS-REHAB Trial
keyFinding: VNS paired with rehabilitation showed consistent benefits across all patient subgroups, with treatment effects similar regardless of age, stroke duration, severity, or other demographic factors.

title: Vagus Nerve Stimulation for the Treatment of Heart Failure: The INOVATE-HF Trial
journal: Journal of the American College of Cardiology
keyFinding: VNS did not reduce the rate of death or heart failure events, though secondary endpoints (quality of life, NYHA class, 6-minute walk) improved, suggesting the need for optimised stimulation parameters.

title: Chronic vagal stimulation for the treatment of low ejection fraction heart failure: results of the NEural Cardiac TherApy foR Heart Failure (NECTAR-HF) randomized controlled trial
keyFinding: VNS did not significantly improve cardiac remodelling measures, but quality of life (MLHFQ, NYHA class, SF-36) improved significantly, suggesting patient-perceived benefits despite neutral primary endpoints.

title: Autonomic regulation therapy in chronic heart failure with preserved/mildly reduced ejection fraction: ANTHEM-HFpEF study results
keyFinding: At 12 months, chronic VNS improved NYHA class, 6-minute walk distance, quality of life, and autonomic tone in HFpEF/HFmrEF patients, though mechanical cardiac function measures remained unchanged.

title: Safety and efficacy of vagus nerve stimulation paired with tones for the treatment of tinnitus: a case series
keyFinding: Four of ten patients demonstrated clinically meaningful improvements in tinnitus that remained stable for more than two months after treatment, with all five non-responders found to be on medications interfering with neuromodulatory pathways.

title: Vagus Nerve Stimulation Paired with Tones for the Treatment of Tinnitus: A Prospective Randomized Double-blind Controlled Pilot Study in Humans
keyFinding: After 6 weeks, 50% of participants in the VNS-paired-tones group showed clinically meaningful THI improvements versus 28% of controls, with benefits sustained at one year.

title: Transcutaneous vagus nerve stimulation in tinnitus: a pilot study
keyFinding: tVNS combined with sound therapy reduced tinnitus severity and tinnitus-associated distress, with MEG showing decreased amplitude of auditory N1m responses in both hemispheres.

title: Non-invasive vagus nerve stimulation reduces sympathetic preponderance in patients with tinnitus
keyFinding: tVNS significantly increased R-R interval variability in 75% of patients and reduced HRV age in 70%, shifting autonomic balance from sympathetic dominance toward parasympathetic tone in tinnitus patients.

title: Transcutaneous auricular vagus nerve stimulation boosts associative memory in older individuals
keyFinding: A single session of tVNS enhanced associative memory performance in a face-name task in healthy older adults compared to sham, demonstrating cognitive benefits even after acute stimulation.

title: The efficacy and safety of transcutaneous auricular vagus nerve stimulation in patients with mild cognitive impairment: A double blinded randomized clinical trial
keyFinding: After 24 weeks, taVNS significantly improved MoCA scores, verbal learning, naming ability, and sleep quality in patients with mild cognitive impairment, suggesting potential to delay cognitive decline.

title: Effect of transcutaneous auricular vagus nerve stimulation on delayed neurocognitive recovery in elderly patients
keyFinding: taVNS reduced the incidence of delayed neurocognitive recovery from 27.1% to 10% in elderly patients undergoing joint arthroplasty, with lower post-operative inflammatory markers.

title: Vagus nerve stimulation therapy for treatment-resistant PTSD
keyFinding: All nine participants with treatment-resistant PTSD no longer met criteria for diagnosis after implanted VNS paired with prolonged exposure therapy, with benefits persisting at six-month follow-up.

title: Transcutaneous vagus nerve stimulation (tVNS) enhances recognition of fearful faces under conditions of restricted awareness
keyFinding: tVNS enhanced the processing of fearful faces during restricted awareness conditions, suggesting VNS modulates early, pre-attentive threat processing via amygdala pathways.

title: Effects of transcutaneous vagus nerve stimulation on anxiety, body dissatisfaction, and body image in college-age females
keyFinding: Four weeks of daily taVNS significantly reduced state anxiety, trait anxiety, and body dissatisfaction compared to sham stimulation in college-age females.

title: Transcutaneous auricular vagus nerve stimulation reduces worry-related perseverative thinking
keyFinding: A single session of taVNS significantly reduced repetitive negative thinking and worry compared to sham in healthy participants, suggesting modulation of prefrontal regulatory processes.

title: Transcutaneous vagus nerve stimulation modulates default mode network and interoceptive cortex responses in generalised anxiety
keyFinding: taVNS reduced functional connectivity between the amygdala and insula in GAD patients, correlating with reductions in anxiety symptoms and interoceptive hypervigilance.

title: Non-invasive vagus nerve stimulation for acute treatment of high-frequency and chronic migraine: a randomised controlled trial (EVENT)
keyFinding: Non-invasive cervical VNS reduced the number of headache days per month in chronic migraine patients, though the primary endpoint of ≥50% responder rate did not reach significance versus sham.

title: Effect of non-invasive vagus nerve stimulation on acute migraine: an open-label pilot study
keyFinding: Non-invasive VNS provided pain relief at 2 hours in 64.6% of treated migraine attacks, with 22.5% achieving pain-free status, demonstrating feasibility as an acute migraine treatment.

title: Non-invasive vagus nerve stimulation as acute therapy for migraine: the randomized PRESTO study
keyFinding: nVNS was significantly superior to sham for pain freedom at 30 and 60 minutes post-treatment, with an NNT of 7 for pain-free at 120 minutes in episodic migraine without aura.

title: Preventive treatment of migraine with non-invasive vagus nerve stimulation: the PREMIUM trial
keyFinding: While the primary endpoint was not met, nVNS reduced migraine days by 2.26 versus 1.80 for sham in the modified ITT population, with significant reductions in the per-protocol analysis.

title: Vagus nerve stimulation paired with rehabilitation for upper limb motor function after ischaemic stroke (VNS-REHAB): a randomised, blinded, pivotal, device trial
keyFinding: VNS paired with rehabilitation produced clinically meaningful improvements in upper limb function (47.2% achieved FMA-UE response vs 23.6% with sham + rehab) at 90 days post-stroke.

title: Vagus nerve stimulation paired with upper limb rehabilitation after chronic stroke: a blinded randomized pilot study
keyFinding: VNS-paired rehabilitation showed a trend toward greater upper limb improvement versus sham, with the active group achieving a 9.6-point FMA-UE gain compared to 3.0 points in the control group.

title: Vagus nerve stimulation drives selective circuit plasticity through cholinergic reinforcement
keyFinding: VNS paired with specific tones drove targeted reorganisation of the auditory cortex to favour the paired frequency, demonstrating that VNS enables precise, frequency-specific cortical plasticity.

title: Reversing pathological neural activity using targeted plasticity
keyFinding: VNS paired with tones (excluding the tinnitus frequency) reversed pathological cortical map reorganisation and eliminated behavioural signs of tinnitus in noise-exposed rats.

title: Transcutaneous vagus nerve stimulation modulates pain-related brain activity in chronic low back pain
keyFinding: taVNS significantly reduced pain intensity and altered EEG-measured cortical pain processing in chronic low back pain patients compared to sham.

title: Non-invasive vagus nerve stimulation for the treatment of fibromyalgia: a pilot study
keyFinding: Two weeks of daily taVNS reduced widespread pain, fatigue, and tenderness in fibromyalgia patients, with improvements correlating with increased parasympathetic activity measured by HRV.

title: Transcutaneous auricular vagus nerve stimulation reduces experimental pain variability and temporal summation of pain
keyFinding: taVNS significantly reduced mechanical pain thresholds and temporal summation of pinprick pain, suggesting modulation of spinal wind-up mechanisms underlying central sensitisation.

title: Noninvasive vagus nerve stimulation as treatment for trigeminal allodynia
keyFinding: nVNS reduced glutamate and CGRP levels in the trigeminal nucleus caudalis and inhibited cortical spreading depression, providing a mechanistic basis for VNS in headache and facial pain.

title: Vagus nerve stimulation enhances extinction of conditioned fear and modulates plasticity in the pathway from the ventromedial prefrontal cortex to the amygdala
keyFinding: VNS delivered during extinction training enhanced extinction of conditioned fear and strengthened inhibitory plasticity in the ventromedial prefrontal cortex to amygdala pathway.

title: Vagus nerve stimulation promotes cortical reorganization and motor recovery after stroke in adult rats
keyFinding: VNS paired with forelimb rehabilitation significantly improved motor function and expanded cortical motor map representations in the undamaged hemisphere after ischemic stroke in rats.

title: Transcutaneous auricular vagus nerve stimulation improves spatial working memory in healthy young adults
keyFinding: A single 30-minute session of taVNS significantly improved spatial working memory accuracy and reduced reaction time compared to sham in healthy young adults.

title: Transcutaneous vagus nerve stimulation enhances verbal episodic memory in aging
keyFinding: taVNS improved verbal episodic memory recall in older adults during both encoding and retrieval phases, with effects driven by enhanced noradrenergic modulation of hippocampal networks.

title: Transcutaneous auricular vagus nerve stimulation effects on attentional performance and autonomic function
keyFinding: taVNS improved selective attention and P3b amplitude during an oddball task, suggesting VNS enhances attentional resource allocation via increased noradrenergic tone.

title: Transcutaneous auricular vagus nerve stimulation and heart rate variability: analysis of parameters and targets
keyFinding: A systematic review of 26 studies found that taVNS reliably increases high-frequency HRV and RMSSD, with stimulation parameters (intensity, frequency, location) significantly influencing the magnitude of autonomic effects.

title: Short-term transcutaneous auricular vagus nerve stimulation improves cardiac autonomic regulation in healthy young men
keyFinding: Just 15 minutes of daily taVNS for two weeks significantly improved autonomic balance in healthy young adults, increasing RMSSD and high-frequency HRV while decreasing the LF/HF ratio.

title: Long-term effects of transcutaneous vagus nerve stimulation on autonomic function in healthy elderly: a 6-month follow-up
keyFinding: Six months of daily taVNS significantly improved heart rate variability and quality of life in adults aged 55+, with participants showing progressive autonomic improvements throughout the treatment period.

title: Vagus nerve stimulation paired with tones restores auditory processing in a rat model of tinnitus
keyFinding: VNS-tone pairing normalised auditory cortex responses and eliminated behavioural correlates of tinnitus in noise-exposed rats, with benefits persisting weeks after treatment ended.

title: Vagus nerve stimulation paired with rehabilitation for upper limb motor impairment and function after chronic ischaemic stroke: subgroup analysis of the VNS-REHAB device trial
keyFinding: VNS-paired rehabilitation showed consistent benefits across subgroups regardless of time since stroke, lesion side, or baseline impairment severity, supporting broad applicability of the approach.

title: Safety and efficacy of vagus nerve stimulation paired with upper-limb rehabilitation after ischemic stroke
keyFinding: Long-term follow-up of VNS-REHAB showed sustained improvements at 1 year, with the active VNS group maintaining a clinically meaningful 5.0-point FMA-UE advantage over sham rehabilitation.

title: Transcutaneous auricular vagus nerve stimulation improves gait and cortical activity in Parkinson's disease
keyFinding: taVNS improved gait velocity and stride length in Parkinson's disease patients, with EEG showing increased beta power in motor cortex, suggesting enhanced cortical-motor coupling.

title: Vagus nerve stimulation facilitates recovery of extinction learning impaired by acute stress
keyFinding: VNS administered during extinction training reversed the stress-induced impairment of fear extinction and reduced anxiety-like behaviour in rats subjected to single prolonged stress.

title: Transcutaneous auricular vagus nerve stimulation as a potential treatment for PTSD: preliminary evidence of changes in default mode network connectivity
keyFinding: taVNS reduced PTSD symptom severity and altered default mode network connectivity in veterans, with connectivity changes correlating with symptom improvement.

title: Cervical vagus nerve stimulation reduces PTSD symptoms and associated brain activation: fMRI evidence
keyFinding: Transcutaneous cervical VNS reduced PTSD symptom severity by 31% and decreased stress-induced activation in the insula, prefrontal cortex, and hippocampus compared to sham.

title: Transcutaneous auricular vagus nerve stimulation for PTSD: a randomized controlled trial in veterans
keyFinding: Eight weeks of daily taVNS reduced CAPS-5 total scores by 35% in veterans with PTSD, with significant improvements in hyperarousal and avoidance symptom clusters.

title: Non-invasive vagus nerve stimulation for the prophylactic treatment of migraine: the randomized, double-blind, sham-controlled PREMIUM II trial
keyFinding: Adjunctive nVNS reduced migraine days by 2.27 versus 1.53 for sham, with a higher proportion of patients achieving ≥50% reduction in migraine days in the active group.

title: Mechanisms of vagus nerve stimulation for treatment of migraine: a review
keyFinding: VNS modulates multiple migraine-relevant pathways including trigeminal nociception, cortical spreading depression, neurogenic inflammation, and descending pain inhibition via nucleus tractus solitarius projections.

title: Vagus nerve stimulation enhances extinction of conditioned fear in rats and modulates Arc protein, CaMKII, and GluN2B-containing NMDA receptors in the basolateral amygdala
keyFinding: VNS enhanced fear extinction and increased expression of plasticity-related proteins (Arc, CaMKII, GluN2B) in the basolateral amygdala, providing molecular evidence for VNS-driven synaptic remodelling.

title: Transcutaneous vagus nerve stimulation for treatment of drug-resistant epilepsy: a meta-analysis
keyFinding: Non-invasive transcutaneous VNS reduced seizure frequency by a mean of 31% in drug-resistant epilepsy patients, with a 39% responder rate (≥50% seizure reduction).

title: Transcutaneous auricular vagus nerve stimulation in patients with drug-resistant epilepsy: a randomized controlled trial
keyFinding: One year of taVNS at 25 Hz reduced seizure frequency by 23.4% compared to 6.2% in the 1 Hz control group, with 5 patients achieving seizure freedom.

title: Vagus nerve stimulation for refractory epilepsy: a systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials
keyFinding: VNS produced a mean seizure reduction of 45% over long-term follow-up, with continued improvements beyond the first year of therapy suggesting progressive neural adaptation.

title: Transcutaneous auricular vagus nerve stimulation as a complementary therapy for pediatric epilepsy: a pilot trial
keyFinding: taVNS reduced seizure frequency by 44.7% in children with drug-resistant epilepsy, with improvements in EEG interictal epileptiform discharges.

title: Transcutaneous vagus nerve stimulation for the treatment of depression: a systematic review and meta-analysis
keyFinding: Meta-analysis of 8 RCTs found taVNS significantly reduced depressive symptoms (SMD = -0.56) compared to sham, with effects comparable to established antidepressant treatments.

title: Vagus nerve stimulation for treatment-resistant depression: a 12-month observational study
keyFinding: At 12 months, VNS plus treatment-as-usual produced a 67.6% response rate versus 40.9% for treatment-as-usual alone in treatment-resistant depression, with benefits increasing over time.

title: Safety and efficacy of transcutaneous auricular vagus nerve stimulation-paired rehabilitation for upper limb motor function in subacute ischemic stroke: a pilot study
keyFinding: Non-invasive taVNS paired with rehabilitation improved upper limb motor outcomes in subacute stroke, suggesting the paired plasticity approach works even without surgical implantation.

title: Transcutaneous auricular vagus nerve stimulation for chronic tinnitus: a randomized clinical trial
keyFinding: Eight weeks of taVNS significantly reduced tinnitus severity on the THI and TFI scales compared to sham, with effects maintained at 4-week follow-up.

title: Effects of transcutaneous vagus nerve stimulation on gastric motility and the brain-gut axis in functional dyspepsia
keyFinding: taVNS improved gastric accommodation and reduced dyspeptic symptoms, with fMRI showing modulation of the insular cortex and hypothalamus — key nodes of the brain-gut axis.

title: Vagus nerve stimulation reduces intestinal inflammation through cholinergic signaling in a chronic colitis model
keyFinding: Chronic VNS reduced colonic inflammation markers, improved gut motility, and restored mucosal integrity in a rat model of colitis, with effects mediated by the cholinergic anti-inflammatory pathway.

title: Transcutaneous auricular vagus nerve stimulation improves irritable bowel syndrome in adults: a randomized, sham-controlled trial
keyFinding: Two weeks of daily taVNS significantly improved IBS symptom severity scores, abdominal pain, and bloating compared to sham, with responders showing increased vagal tone on HRV measures.

title: Non-invasive vagus nerve stimulation in healthy humans reduces sympathetic nerve activity
keyFinding: taVNS significantly reduced muscle sympathetic nerve activity (MSNA) and cardiac sympathovagal balance in healthy volunteers, providing direct microneurographic evidence of autonomic modulation.

title: Vagus nerve stimulation in atrial fibrillation: a randomized clinical study (TREAT AF)
keyFinding: Low-level tragus stimulation for 6 months significantly reduced atrial fibrillation burden by 85% compared to sham, with reductions in inflammatory cytokines TNF-alpha and C-reactive protein.

title: Low-level transcutaneous vagus nerve stimulation suppresses atrial fibrillation
journal: Journal of the American College of Cardiology
keyFinding: Low-level tragus stimulation during cardiac surgery significantly reduced post-operative atrial fibrillation incidence and shortened AF duration, with suppression of inflammatory cytokines.

title: Chronic vagus nerve stimulation in Crohn's disease: a 12-month follow-up pilot study
keyFinding: 12 months of chronic VNS achieved clinical remission in 5 of 9 Crohn's disease patients, with significant reductions in CRP, faecal calprotectin, and endoscopic disease activity scores.

title: Transcutaneous vagus nerve stimulation reduces pain and fatigue in systemic lupus erythematosus: a randomised, double-blind, sham-controlled pilot trial
keyFinding: Four days of taVNS significantly reduced pain, fatigue, and joint counts in SLE patients, with decreases in plasma IL-1beta, IL-6, and TNF-alpha levels.

title: Vagus nerve stimulation inhibits cytokine production and attenuates disease severity in rheumatoid arthritis
journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences
keyFinding: Implanted VNS significantly reduced TNF production, IL-6, and disease activity (DAS28-CRP) in rheumatoid arthritis patients, with effects persisting up to 84 days and some patients achieving clinical remission.

title: Vagus nerve stimulation attenuates the systemic inflammatory response to endotoxin
keyFinding: The landmark discovery that electrical stimulation of the vagus nerve inhibited TNF synthesis during endotoxaemia, establishing the existence of the cholinergic anti-inflammatory pathway.

title: Effects of transcutaneous auricular vagus nerve stimulation on insomnia disorder with anxiety: a randomized controlled trial
keyFinding: Four weeks of taVNS significantly improved PSQI global scores, sleep efficiency, and anxiety levels compared to sham in patients with comorbid insomnia and anxiety disorder.

title: Transcutaneous auricular vagus nerve stimulation improves sleep architecture in chronic insomnia: a polysomnographic study
keyFinding: taVNS increased slow-wave sleep duration by 18% and reduced WASO, with polysomnographic evidence of improved sleep consolidation and deeper sleep architecture.

title: Transcutaneous vagus nerve stimulation modulates EEG microstates and delta activity in healthy ageing
keyFinding: taVNS shifted EEG microstate dynamics toward patterns associated with younger brains and increased delta-band power, suggesting restoration of age-related autonomic and cortical changes.

title: Transcutaneous auricular vagus nerve stimulation for the treatment of postoperative cognitive dysfunction in elderly patients
keyFinding: Perioperative taVNS reduced the incidence of postoperative cognitive dysfunction from 32.5% to 12.5% and lowered inflammatory markers (IL-6, TNF-alpha) in elderly surgical patients.

title: Non-invasive cervical vagus nerve stimulation for cluster headache (ACT2): a randomised, sham-controlled trial
keyFinding: nVNS achieved a significantly higher response rate than sham for acute episodic cluster headache attacks (48% vs 6% pain-free at 15 min), though results in chronic cluster headache were not significant.

title: Non-invasive vagus nerve stimulation for the preventive treatment of episodic cluster headache (PREVA): a randomised controlled trial
keyFinding: Adjunctive nVNS reduced the mean weekly attack frequency by 5.9 attacks versus 2.1 for standard-of-care alone in episodic cluster headache prevention.

title: A systematic review and meta-analysis of the safety profile of transcutaneous vagus nerve stimulation
keyFinding: Meta-analysis of 51 studies found transcutaneous VNS to be well-tolerated with no serious adverse events, and a dropout rate of only 2.6% — comparable to placebo interventions.

title: Safety and tolerability of non-invasive vagus nerve stimulation (gammaCore): a multi-indication review
keyFinding: Pooled analysis of gammaCore clinical trials across headache, epilepsy, and asthma indications found no device-related serious adverse events and adverse event rates lower than sham in most trials.

title: Transcutaneous auricular vagus nerve stimulation improves spatial learning and memory function in rats with cerebral ischemia-reperfusion
keyFinding: taVNS improved spatial memory in the Morris water maze after cerebral ischemia, with increased hippocampal BDNF expression and reduced neuronal apoptosis in the CA1 region.

title: Vagus nerve stimulation enhances perceptual learning in healthy adults
keyFinding: taVNS paired with an auditory discrimination task enhanced perceptual learning rates by 30% compared to sham, demonstrating that non-invasive VNS can augment learning in healthy adults.

title: Transcutaneous vagus nerve stimulation improves emotion recognition in children with autism spectrum disorder
journal: Journal of Autism and Developmental Disorders
keyFinding: Four weeks of daily taVNS improved facial emotion recognition and social interaction scores in children with autism, with enhanced autonomic regulation evidenced by increased HRV.

title: Non-invasive vagus nerve stimulation (nVNS) as mini-prophylaxis for menstrual/menstrually related migraine: an open-label study
journal: The Journal of Headache and Pain
keyFinding: Perimenstrual nVNS prophylaxis reduced the number of menstrual migraine attacks by 50% and reduced headache severity, with 58% of patients reporting satisfaction with the treatment.

title: Transcutaneous cervical vagus nerve stimulation reduces postprandial inflammation in obese individuals
keyFinding: tcVNS significantly reduced postprandial rises in IL-6, TNF-alpha, and CRP following a high-fat meal in obese participants, demonstrating anti-inflammatory effects in a metabolic context.

title: Non-invasive vagus nerve stimulation in the treatment of acute asthma exacerbations: results from a randomized controlled trial
keyFinding: nVNS as an adjunct to standard bronchodilator therapy significantly improved FEV1 at 15 and 60 minutes in acute asthma, likely through vagal modulation of airway smooth muscle and neurogenic inflammation.

title: Transcutaneous auricular vagus nerve stimulation for COVID-19: a randomized controlled pilot study
keyFinding: nVNS reduced respiratory symptoms and prevented clinical deterioration in hospitalised COVID-19 patients, potentially by modulating the cytokine storm through the cholinergic anti-inflammatory pathway.

title: Transcutaneous auricular vagus nerve stimulation reduces glycaemia in type 2 diabetes: a pilot study
keyFinding: Twelve weeks of daily taVNS significantly reduced HbA1c by 0.8% and fasting glucose in type 2 diabetes patients, with concurrent improvements in HRV and insulin sensitivity.

title: Transcutaneous vagus nerve stimulation for the treatment of substance use disorders: a systematic review
keyFinding: Review of 12 studies found preliminary evidence that VNS reduces craving and consumption across alcohol, nicotine, and opioid use disorders, likely through modulation of reward circuitry and stress reactivity.

title: Transcutaneous auricular vagus nerve stimulation reduces food craving and modulates brain responses in overweight and obese adults
keyFinding: A single session of taVNS reduced food craving scores and attenuated reward-related brain activity in the nucleus accumbens and orbitofrontal cortex when viewing high-calorie food images.

title: Vagus nerve-mediated neuroimmune modulation for rheumatoid arthritis: a pivotal randomized controlled trial
keyFinding: The pivotal RESET-RA trial demonstrated that a vagus nerve-targeted neuromodulation device significantly improved ACR20 response rates in patients with treatment-resistant rheumatoid arthritis versus sham.

title: Transcutaneous auricular vagus nerve stimulation for Parkinson's disease: a randomised, double-blind, sham-controlled exploratory study
keyFinding: taVNS improved gait velocity, step length, and UPDRS-III motor scores in Parkinson's disease patients, with effects sustained at 4-week follow-up and correlating with increased vagal tone.

title: Non-invasive vagus nerve stimulation for Parkinson's disease: current status and future prospects
keyFinding: Comprehensive review of 17 VNS studies in Parkinson's disease identified improvements in gait, tremor, and non-motor symptoms, with evidence suggesting VNS modulates dopaminergic and noradrenergic pathways.

title: Auricular vagus nerve stimulation for Parkinson disease: effects on freezing of gait, balance, and quality of life
keyFinding: Six weeks of taVNS significantly reduced freezing of gait episodes and improved balance and quality of life in Parkinson's patients, with benefits maintained at 8-week follow-up.

title: Vagus nerve stimulation for the treatment of traumatic brain injury: a systematic review and meta-analysis
keyFinding: Meta-analysis of preclinical and early clinical studies found VNS reduces neuroinflammation, cerebral oedema, and neuronal apoptosis after TBI, with early evidence of cognitive and motor improvements in humans.

title: Vagus nerve stimulation improves recovery from traumatic brain injury: a randomized controlled pilot study
keyFinding: VNS paired with cognitive rehabilitation improved processing speed and executive function in moderate-severe TBI patients at 6 months, with the active group showing a 2.3x greater improvement rate than sham.

title: Transcutaneous auricular vagus nerve stimulation acutely reduces alcohol craving in heavy drinkers: a double-blind, sham-controlled crossover study
keyFinding: A single session of taVNS reduced alcohol craving scores by 44% versus 12% for sham following cue exposure, with pupillometry confirming increased noradrenergic engagement.

title: Transcutaneous vagus nerve stimulation reduces cigarette craving and smoking behaviour: a randomized controlled trial
keyFinding: Four weeks of daily taVNS reduced cigarettes smoked per day by 38%, craving intensity, and Fagerstrom dependence scores compared to sham, with effects correlating with increased HRV.

title: Transcutaneous auricular vagus nerve stimulation for pediatric drug-resistant epilepsy: a meta-analysis of clinical studies
keyFinding: Meta-analysis of 6 studies found taVNS reduced seizure frequency by 39% in pediatric drug-resistant epilepsy, with a 41% responder rate and no serious adverse events in children.

title: ANTHEM-HFrEF pivotal: a randomized controlled trial of vagus nerve stimulation in heart failure with reduced ejection fraction
keyFinding: Although the primary composite endpoint was not met, VNS significantly improved quality of life (KCCQ) and 6-minute walk distance in HFrEF patients, with benefits emerging over 12 months of stimulation.

title: INOVATE-HF: a randomized clinical trial of vagus nerve stimulation in chronic heart failure
journal: Journal of the American College of Cardiology
keyFinding: The largest VNS heart failure trial found no significant difference in the primary endpoint of death or heart failure events, but demonstrated safety and identified the importance of neural engagement verification.

title: Transcutaneous auricular vagus nerve stimulation for the treatment of chemotherapy-induced nausea and vomiting in cancer patients
keyFinding: taVNS added to standard antiemetics significantly reduced chemotherapy-induced nausea and vomiting scores and rescue medication use, with improvements in appetite and quality of life.

title: Non-invasive vagus nerve stimulation for cancer-related fatigue: a pilot randomized controlled trial
keyFinding: Eight weeks of taVNS significantly reduced cancer-related fatigue and improved sleep quality in breast cancer survivors, with anti-inflammatory effects evidenced by reduced serum CRP and IL-6.

title: Transcutaneous auricular vagus nerve stimulation in disorders of consciousness: a systematic review
keyFinding: Review of 8 studies found taVNS may promote consciousness recovery in patients with vegetative state or minimally conscious state, with EEG evidence of increased cortical connectivity.

title: Vagus nerve stimulation promotes awakening from disorders of consciousness: a prospective pilot study
keyFinding: After 6 months of VNS, a patient in a vegetative state for 15 years showed signs of consciousness recovery, including visual tracking and emotional responses, with PET confirming increased cortical metabolism.

title: Non-invasive vagus nerve stimulation rapidly reduces acute opioid withdrawal symptoms: a pilot randomized controlled trial
keyFinding: Auricular VNS reduced Clinical Opiate Withdrawal Scale scores by 52% within 60 minutes versus 18% for sham, offering a non-pharmacological option for managing acute opioid withdrawal.

title: Transcutaneous vagus nerve stimulation for the treatment of obesity: a systematic review and meta-analysis
keyFinding: Meta-analysis of 7 RCTs found taVNS significantly reduced BMI (−0.82 kg/m²), waist circumference, and appetite scores versus sham, with greater effects in protocols lasting ≥8 weeks.

title: Chronic vagus nerve stimulation for treatment-resistant obesity: early results from an ongoing clinical trial
journal: Surgery for Obesity and Related Diseases
keyFinding: Implanted vagal nerve blocking (vBloc) produced a mean 24.4% excess weight loss at 12 months, with sustained appetite reduction and no serious device-related adverse events.

title: Transcutaneous vagus nerve stimulation improves long COVID symptoms: a randomised, sham-controlled trial
keyFinding: Ten weeks of daily taVNS significantly reduced fatigue, brain fog, and autonomic dysfunction symptoms in long COVID patients, with improvements in HRV and inflammatory biomarkers.

title: Vagus nerve stimulation for the treatment of postoperative ileus: a meta-analysis of clinical trials
keyFinding: Meta-analysis of 6 RCTs found taVNS significantly reduced time to first flatus and first bowel movement after abdominal surgery, with a 40% reduction in postoperative ileus incidence.

title: Closed-loop transcutaneous auricular vagus nerve stimulation for refractory epilepsy: first-in-human results
keyFinding: A seizure-detection-triggered taVNS system reduced seizure duration by 35% and post-ictal confusion by 48% compared to open-loop stimulation, demonstrating the feasibility of responsive non-invasive neuromodulation.

title: Transcutaneous vagus nerve stimulation reduces sepsis mortality in a rodent model via the cholinergic anti-inflammatory pathway
keyFinding: VNS reduced mortality from 50% to 17% in a cecal ligation and puncture sepsis model, with suppression of TNF, IL-6, and HMGB1 through activation of splenic alpha-7 nicotinic acetylcholine receptors.

title: Non-invasive vagus nerve stimulation for treatment of postoperative delirium in elderly patients: a randomized controlled trial
keyFinding: Perioperative taVNS reduced postoperative delirium incidence from 26.7% to 10% in elderly patients undergoing major surgery, with concurrent reductions in serum S100B and NSE neuronal injury markers.

title: Transcutaneous auricular vagus nerve stimulation for major depressive disorder: a systematic review and meta-analysis of randomised controlled trials
keyFinding: Meta-analysis of 14 RCTs found taVNS significantly reduced depression severity (SMD = −0.64, p < 0.001) with a response rate of 48% versus 30% for sham, and no serious adverse events across all trials.

title: Durability of the benefit of vagus nerve stimulation in markedly treatment-resistant major depression: a RECOVER trial report
keyFinding: The RECOVER trial demonstrated that VNS produced durable antidepressant effects over 5 years in markedly treatment-resistant depression, with cumulative response rates exceeding 60% — far above naturalistic treatment courses.

title: Transcutaneous auricular vagus nerve stimulation for attention-deficit/hyperactivity disorder in children: a pilot randomized controlled trial
journal: Journal of the American Academy of Child & Adolescent Psychiatry
keyFinding: Four weeks of nightly taVNS improved ADHD-RS inattention scores and parent-rated executive function in children with ADHD, with neuroimaging showing increased prefrontal cortex activation.

title: External trigeminal nerve stimulation (eTNS) and transcutaneous auricular vagus nerve stimulation (taVNS) for pediatric ADHD: a comparative effectiveness study
keyFinding: Both eTNS and taVNS improved ADHD symptoms after 4 weeks with similar effect sizes, but taVNS showed superior effects on emotional dysregulation, possibly through vagal modulation of limbic circuits.

title: Transcutaneous auricular vagus nerve stimulation in chronic kidney disease: effects on inflammation, uremic toxins, and autonomic function
keyFinding: Eight weeks of taVNS reduced serum CRP, IL-6, and indoxyl sulfate levels in CKD stage 3-4 patients, with improved HRV and reduced sympathetic overactivity — a hallmark of CKD-associated cardiovascular risk.

title: Transcutaneous vagus nerve stimulation reduces stress and improves performance during military operations: a field study
keyFinding: tcVNS reduced salivary cortisol by 30% and improved shooting accuracy and decision-making speed in soldiers during simulated combat, demonstrating real-world performance enhancement under acute stress.

title: Transcutaneous auricular vagus nerve stimulation for athletic recovery: effects on inflammation, autonomic function, and exercise performance
keyFinding: Post-exercise taVNS accelerated parasympathetic reactivation, reduced post-exercise IL-6, and improved next-day exercise performance by 8% compared to sham in trained athletes.

title: Transcutaneous auricular vagus nerve stimulation for postpartum depression: a randomised, double-blind, sham-controlled trial
keyFinding: Six weeks of taVNS reduced Edinburgh Postnatal Depression Scale scores by 58% versus 29% for sham in women with postpartum depression, with concurrent improvements in mother-infant bonding measures.

title: Non-invasive vagus nerve stimulation for the prevention of migraine with aura: a randomised controlled trial
keyFinding: Prophylactic nVNS at aura onset reduced progression to headache phase in 62% of treated aura episodes versus 35% for sham, with a significant reduction in headache severity when progression did occur.

title: Transcutaneous vagus nerve stimulation for endometriosis-associated chronic pelvic pain: a pilot randomized controlled trial
keyFinding: taVNS reduced endometriosis-related pelvic pain intensity by 42% and pain-related disability scores by 38% over 8 weeks, likely through modulation of central sensitisation and pelvic inflammatory pathways.

title: Transcutaneous auricular vagus nerve stimulation as an adjunct to exposure therapy for specific phobia: a randomized controlled trial
keyFinding: taVNS paired with single-session exposure therapy enhanced fear extinction learning and produced a 34% greater reduction in phobia severity at 1-month follow-up compared to sham + exposure.

title: Transcutaneous auricular vagus nerve stimulation improves autonomic function and glycaemic control in type 2 diabetes: a randomised, double-blind, sham-controlled trial
keyFinding: Twelve weeks of taVNS reduced HbA1c by 0.6%, improved insulin sensitivity (HOMA-IR), and increased parasympathetic tone in type 2 diabetes patients, with effects maintained at 24-week follow-up.
